caption: 2012 financial model under Rory Read: 44-48% gross margin target, ~$610M quarterly opex, operating income targeted merely "positive", ~$200M capex - the pre-Zen baseline AMD.
Portfolio triage at the trough: exit SeaMicro dense servers, low-end tablets and smartphones; concentrate investment on high-performance cores, gaming and datacenter.
First public Zen disclosure: all-new high-performance x86 core with SMT and FinFET, scaling from client to enterprise, availability 2016.
Long-term targets set at the trough: 36-40% gross margin, opex 26-30% of revenue, >10% operating margin, >$0.50 EPS - goals AMD later exceeded by an order of magnitude.

WSA 6th amendment economics: ~$335M Q3-16 charge ($100M cash plus 75M-share Mubadala warrant) plus per-wafer payments to GF on outside-foundry purchases - the sourcing flexibility that preceded the move of CPUs to TSMC.
FAD 2017 long-term model: double-digit revenue growth, 40-44% gross margin, >$0.75 EPS - the first post-Zen profitability frame.
EPYC "Naples" server re-entry spec: 32 Zen cores, 8 memory channels, 128 PCIe lanes per socket - the no-compromise one-socket disruption pitch against Intel Xeon.
FAD 2020 TAM: $79B total (Data Center $35B, PCs $32B, Gaming $12B) - the opportunity set as management framed it before Xilinx and before the AI buildout.
FAD 2020 long-term model: ~20% revenue CAGR, >50% gross margin, opex 26-27% of revenue, mid-20s operating margin, >15% FCF margin - subsequently achieved or beaten on every line.
Mix target: Data Center from 15% of 2019 revenue to >30% long-term; actual 2025 mix reached ~47% - share-gain compounding ran ahead of management plan.
FAD 2022 TAM: ~$300B (Data Center $125B, PCs $50B, Gaming $37B, Embedded $33B, Communications $32B, Automotive $27B) - up from $79B in 2020 after Xilinx and Pensando.
2019-2021 delivery: gross margin 43% to 48%, operating margin 12% to 25%, EPS $0.64 to $2.79, FCF $0.3B to $3.2B - the proof points behind the Xilinx-era model.
MI300 pre-launch: first data center APU, CDNA3 plus Zen 4 with unified on-package memory, >8x expected AI training vs MI250X, available 2023 - the origin slide of the Instinct AI franchise.
FAD 2022 long-term model (~3-4yr): ~20% CAGR off 2021 pro forma, >57% gross margin, 23-24% opex, mid-30s operating margin, >25% FCF margin - the gross margin target the AI era later walked back.
"Uniquely positioned in AI" portfolio map: MI250/MI300, 4th-gen EPYC, first x86 NPU in Ryzen 7040, Versal AI Edge, Alveo - CPU+GPU+FPGA+NPU breadth as the AI claim before the MI300 ramp.
ZT Systems close (March 31, 2025): rack-scale systems design named a key factor in scaling Instinct; manufacturing pre-agreed for sale to Sanmina - systems capability without factory capital.
Server CPU revenue share ~40% (Mercury Q2 2025) across five EPYC generations; Data Center revenue $2B / 17% of total (2020) to >$16B / ~47% (2025e).
2016-2025e non-GAAP arc: revenue $4B to ~$34B, gross profit $1B to ~$18B, operating profit $43M to ~$8B (79% CAGR) - the turnaround quantified end to end.
Data center TAM claim: ~$200B 2025 to >$1T 2030 (>40% CAGR) - the demand assumption underneath every FAD 2025 target.
Anchor deals: OpenAI 6GW of Instinct starting 2H26, 50,000-GPU MI450 zetta-scale deployment, Helios rack co-defined with Meta, Lux (MI355X) and Discovery (MI430X) US AI factories.
Data Center AI revenue underwrite: >80% CAGR next 3-5 years, on-track to tens of billions of dollars in 2027, multiple GW-scale hyperscaler, AI-native and sovereign opportunities.
Share targets next 3-5 years: >50% server revenue share, >40% client revenue share, >70% adaptive revenue share, >80% DC AI revenue CAGR - a majority-share endgame vs Intel stated explicitly.
AI adds ~$30B of server CPU demand by 2030: DC CPU TAM $26B 2025 to ~$60B 2030 (AI-driven ~18% CAGR vs 4% general compute) - the CPU-attach thesis for AI clusters.
MI450 rack vs Nvidia Vera Rubin (AMD engineering projections): 1.5x memory capacity and bandwidth, ~1x flops, 1.5x scale-out bandwidth - memory-led positioning for inference.
Helios scale-up architecture: 72 GPUs per rack, 3.6TB/s per-GPU bandwidth, 260TB/s aggregate, shared pod memory acting as a single GPU, six parallel network planes on open Ethernet (ESUN).
Client momentum 2023-2025e: revenue market share 15% to 20% to ~28%, processor ASP 1x to 1.1x to ~1.5x, client revenue $5B to $7B to >$10B - premium mix, not just units.
Client revenue share target >40% by 2030 (from ~28% 2025e), growing >3x market rate - the PC share-gain runway management is underwriting.
Embedded trajectory: grow 2x market rate through 2030, accelerating to 3x with TAM expansion; FPGA share ~55% 2025 to >70% target 2030.
FAD 2025 long-term model: >35% revenue CAGR, 55-58% gross margin, >35% operating margin, 13-15% tax, ~25% FCF margin, >$20 EPS over 3-5 years.
Helios at CES 2026: 43TB/s scale-out bandwidth, 31TB HBM4, 4,600 Zen 6 cores, 18,000 GPU compute units, 2.9 exaflops AI compute per rack on 2nm/3nm - the rack as the unit of product.
